details.description: Coastal panorama at dusk showing the famous limestone sea stacks known as the Twelve Apostles along Victoria’s Great Ocean Road. Multiple tall, stratified pillars of pale-gold limestone rise from the Southern Ocean, with waves breaking around their bases. To the right, high, horizontally banded coastal cliffs continue into the distance above a narrow sandy beach. The light is soft, with a pink-orange gradient sky indicating sunset or twilight. The viewpoint appears to be from a cliff-top lookout platform above the beach, looking along the coastline where several stacks are aligned offshore.

details.description: A coastal panorama at sunset showing a line of limestone sea stacks rising from the calm ocean, aligned parallel to a rugged cliff face on the right. The sky is painted in soft pastel pinks and peaches, reflecting on the water and gently washing the shore with light. The foreground reveals a sandy beach and shallow waves, while distant stacks recede toward the horizon, forming the iconic Twelve Apostles sequence in Victoria, Australia. The scene is characteristic of the Port Campbell National Park coastline along the Great Ocean Road, with dramatic limestone formations carved by erosion over millennia.

details.description: Coastal landscape at dusk showing a line of tall, freestanding limestone sea stacks rising from the surf beside high stratified cliffs and a narrow sandy beach. Gentle waves of a blue‑grey ocean wash around the bases of the stacks, and the sky is bathed in soft pink and orange twilight hues. The viewpoint appears to be a cliff‑top lookout along the Great Ocean Road, looking west along the coast toward multiple stacks of the Twelve Apostles within Port Campbell National Park, Victoria, Australia.

The sky fades from pale blue to pink and orange, with a narrow sandy beach and gentle waves in the foreground; distant headlands and more stacks recede toward the horizon.

details.description: A scenic coastal landscape captured at twilight, likely sunset, featuring towering limestone stacks rising from the ocean. The sky displays a soft gradient of pastel pinks, oranges, and blues. To the right, a massive, sheer cliff face of golden-colored limestone extends into the distance, with a sandy beach visible at its base where waves are crashing. In the water, several rock formations are prominent: a large, blocky stack in the middle ground, a taller, more slender stack behind it, and smaller rock remnants in the foreground and background. The ocean is calm with rolling waves creating white foam patterns on the surface. The viewpoint is elevated, looking along the coastline.

details.description: A scenic coastal landscape at dusk or dawn, featuring several prominent limestone stacks rising from the Southern Ocean. The sky is painted with soft pastel hues of pink, orange, and light blue. To the right, steep, layered cliffs of golden-brown limestone drop sharply to a narrow sandy beach where waves are washing ashore. The water is a deep blue with white foam from the breaking waves around the base of the stacks. The lighting is soft and diffused, characteristic of the golden hour, highlighting the textures of the rock formations and the coastal cliffs.

details.description: Coastal panorama at sunset showing a line of tall limestone sea stacks standing just offshore from high stratified cliffs and a narrow sandy beach. Gentle surf wraps around the stacks and the base of the cliffs. The warm, layered rock faces and isolated pillars are characteristic of the Twelve Apostles along Victoria’s Shipwreck Coast. Sky is pastel pink and blue, suggesting dusk or dawn light.

details.description: A serene coastal sunset scene along Victoria's Great Ocean Road, showing multiple limestone sea stacks rising from calm blue-green water near a rugged cliff. The foreground features a tall, stratified limestone cliff on the right with visible horizontal layers. Offshore, several weathered stacks of varying heights stand in a line, with a sandy beach and gentle surf along the base. The sky glows with pastel pinks, peaches, and pale blue as the sun sets on the horizon, casting warm light on the rock faces and creating reflections on the water.

details.description: A coastal landscape at sunset featuring a sequence of limestone sea stacks rising from the calm sea near a sandy beach. The sky is painted in soft pastel hues of pink, orange, and blue. To the right, a tall, layered cliff runs along the shore, with some vegetation on top. Gentle waves lap the shore, leaving wet, reflective sand near the waterline, while distant landforms line the horizon.

entities.name: Sea stacks (limestone rock formations) along the coastline
entities.notes: Multiple vertical rock pillars rising from the sea, evidence of coastal erosion.

entities.name: Cliffs with sedimentary layering
entities.notes: Steep cliff face on the right side with visible stratification and some vegetation on top.

details.description: A scenic coastal view of the Twelve Apostles, a collection of limestone stacks rising from the Southern Ocean. The image captures the scene during twilight, likely sunset, with a soft gradient sky transitioning from pale blue to pastel pink and orange near the horizon. Several prominent rock formations stand isolated in the water, showing distinct horizontal layering from geological erosion. To the right, a massive, sheer limestone cliff face borders a sandy beach where gentle waves are breaking. The water is a muted blue-grey, reflecting the sky's soft light. The perspective looks along the coastline, highlighting the dramatic separation of the stacks from the mainland cliffs.

details.description: A scenic coastal landscape featuring several large limestone stacks, known as the Twelve Apostles, rising from the Southern Ocean. The stacks are situated near a high, vertical cliff face along a sandy beach. The sky is illuminated with soft pink, orange, and blue hues of a sunset or sunrise. The water shows white foam from waves breaking around the base of the stacks and along the shoreline. The lighting is soft and warm, highlighting the layered texture of the limestone.
